narrative: Iran war ongoing since Feb 28; Brent $107–112, WTI $102–108; Hormuz mostly closed. Fed 3.50–3.75%, PCE 2.7% (sticky). S&P 500 ATH 7,501 (May 15), fwd P/E ~21x. BRK Q1 2026 13F: $263B portfolio, $397B cash, GOOGL tripled.

macro.regime: Stagflation re-emerging — oil shock + tariff persistence + sticky inflation
macro.geopolitics: Iran war ongoing since Feb 28; Hormuz mostly closed; fragile ceasefire.

discount_premium.what: Adds to the 10% base discount rate. Higher → future cash flows are worth less today.
discount_premium.raise_when: Rising real yields, widening risk premia, geopolitical shocks, late-cycle valuations.
discount_premium.lower_when: Fed cutting decisively, falling yields, low credit spreads, early-cycle.
discount_premium.today: Oil shock + sticky 2.7% PCE + ISM prices 84.6 + 10-yr ~4.5% → premium ON. Stagflation argues for a higher real discount rate (default 1.3% is light; 2.5–4% defensible).

growth_haircut.what: Subtracts from each ticker's starting growth before the DCF compounds it 15 years.
growth_haircut.raise_when: Stagflation, tariff/supply-chain hits, weakening consumer, energy spikes for non-energy names.
growth_haircut.lower_when: Acceleration, productivity boom, falling input costs, expansionary fiscal/monetary.
growth_haircut.today: Tariff regime persists post-IEEPA ruling; oil hurts margins. Default -0.5% haircut is mild; -1 to -2% reasonable for cyclicals.

mos_add.what: Margin of safety added on top of Buffett's base 25%. Bigger MOS = more conservative IV.
mos_add.raise_when: Valuations stretched (S&P fwd P/E ≥ 20), late-cycle, regime change in progress, fat tails.
mos_add.lower_when: Cheap broad market, panic discounts, high-conviction quality at a known floor.
mos_add.today: S&P at ATH 7,501, fwd P/E ~21x, war ongoing. Default +4.0% MOS is appropriate; +6–10% if you want more cushion.

global_growth_shift.what: Adds (or subtracts) a constant to every ticker's revenue growth before the DCF.
global_growth_shift.raise_when: You believe consensus growth is too pessimistic across the board.
global_growth_shift.lower_when: You expect a synchronized slowdown or recession (e.g. shift everything -1 to -3%).
global_growth_shift.today: Recession-probability surveys ~28% (WSJ). Try shifting -1% to stress-test, or 0 if you trust per-ticker.

dcf_input_notes.discount_rate: Cost of equity. 10% is the long-run S&P nominal average. Adjusted up per sector by the geopolitical overlay below.
dcf_input_notes.terminal_growth: Sustainable long-run growth from year 15 onward. 2.5% sits at the conservative end of nominal GDP — should not exceed long-run nominal GDP.
dcf_input_notes.projection_years: DCF horizon. Buffett-style 15 years; long enough that growth that fades early is correctly penalized.
dcf_input_notes.margin_of_safety: Buffett's bargain margin — buy at a discount to fair value. 25% base; the geopolitical overlay adds more in stressed regimes.
